description_long: This house features a sprawling, single-story layout with a red-tiled hip and gable roof, characteristic of suburban ranch-style homes. The exterior walls are finished in red brick, complemented by large, rectangular windows that provide ample natural light to the interior. The front facade includes a prominent entryway with a small overhang and steps leading up to the main door, flanked by multiple windows. The structure extends in an L-shaped configuration, with one wing projecting forward and another stretching back, creating a sense of depth and separation between living spaces. The roofline is varied, with intersecting hips and gables adding architectural interest and visual complexity. The house sits low to the ground, emphasizing its horizontal proportions and blending seamlessly into a typical residential neighborhood.
description: A sprawling, single-story red brick house with a red-tiled hip and gable roof, large windows, and an L-shaped layout. The entryway features a small overhang and steps, giving the home a classic suburban ranch appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entryway with a small overhang, steps, and several large windows along the facade.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the depth of the house, showing the projecting wing and the varied roofline with intersecting hips and gables.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals more windows and the full extent of the L-shaped layout, with the long wing stretching back.
description_view_3: This opposite side view emphasizes the length of the house and the consistent use of red brick and tiled roofing.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with a small overhang, steps, and a prominent door flanked by windows
